Question
========
Which of the following statements about the R console are correct?

Answerlist
----------
* The console functions as a terminal for the R language
* The presence of $>$ indicates the computer is awaiting your command
* Console history is preserved when you close and re-open RStudio
* Typing commands into the console is useful for quick tasks you don't need to preserve
* Scripts are unnecessary if you use the console regularly

Solution
========


Answerlist
----------
* True. The text states "The console pane functions as a terminal for the R language specifically."
* True. The text says "The presence of $>$ indicates that the computer is awaiting your command."
* False. The text states "you will find that any history of this calculation is gone when you re-open the environment."
* True. The text mentions the console "offers us a quick way to perform simple tasks that we are not necessarily concerned with preserving."
* False. The text emphasizes that scripts become important for code "we do want to preserve, run, edit, and add to at later date."

Meta-information
================
exname: console_properties
extype: mchoice
exsolution: 11010
exshuffle: TRUE
